DEMURRER to CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jul-25-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Jordan Greenberg, Esq. (847) 826-7825 jordangreenberglaw@gmail.com Attorney for Cross-Defendants James Zordani and Armen Tatevosian (via ZOOM)
Brandon K. Franklin, Esq., SEYFARTH SHAW LLP (415) 397-2823 Attorneys for Defendants
GIGSTER, INC., GIGSTER, LLC, IONIC PARTNERS, ANDREW TRYBA, JUN HO SON, CHRIS KEENE and PHIL FASANO

Hearing is held. ARGUED. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

CROSS DEFENDANT JAMES ZORDANI AND ARMEN TATEVOSIAN's DEMURRER to CROSS COMPLAINT.

Cross-defendants' demurrer to the cross-complaint (XC) is overruled.

Cross-defendants argue that the trade-secret-related claims are not pled with "reasonable particularity." (Memo. 3:17, 7:8, 7:9, 8:22; see also Not. 2:8, 2:13, 2:27, 3:5.) However, that term regards not pleadings but rather the CCP 2019.210 trade secret disclosure that precedes discovery. (Id.; Brescia v. Angelin (2009) 172 Cal.App.4th 133, 144.) In any event, the 23-page cross-complaint adequately pleads the elements of its causes of action.
It is also argued that conspiracy and contract breach are not adequately alleged (Not. 2:9-22), but they are. (XC 13:15-18:11.)

Finally, it is argued that the UCL claim is preempted by the misappropriation claim because they rely on the same facts. (Not. 3:5-7.) But parties "may plead in the alternative." (Adams v. Paul (1995) 11 Cal.4th 583, 593.)

Order is signed

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (302/RBU)
